mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is flat or falling, lower discount rates and easier liquidity support equity multiples, so the S&P 500 should post positive 20-day forward returns. The signal is strongest when CPIAUCSL is not accelerating, because falling policy rates then transmit mainly through valuation rather than inflation fear.
failure_condition: Rejected if 20-day SPX returns are not positive on average when FEDFUNDS is declining or unchanged, or if the hit rate is below 55% after controlling for CPIAUCSL direction.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above recent levels signals deteriorating labor demand and rising recession risk, which should weaken near-term equity returns as analysts cut earnings expectations. This mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next month after the unemployment rate crosses a short-run threshold.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Rejected if triggered UNRATE deterioration events are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average forward return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous acceleration in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates broadening payroll income and retail spending, which should lift revenue expectations and support equities. The effect is interaction-driven because stronger labor income without stronger consumption, or vice versa, is insufficient to generate the same positive return signal.
failure_condition: Rejected if the joint condition PAYEMS growth and RSAFS growth does not outperform either series alone in predicting positive 60-day SPX returns, or if interaction terms are insignificant in rolling tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO relative to prior months signals improving industrial output and cyclical momentum, which should translate into positive equity returns over the next week as investors reprice growth-sensitive sectors. The signal is intended as a fast trend-following proxy for real activity rather than a long-lag recession indicator.
failure_condition: Rejected if positive week-ahead SPX returns do not occur more often than negative returns following INDPRO monthly increases, or if the rolling 1-year information coefficient is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A housing-starts rebound after weakness signals improving rate-sensitive domestic demand and a stabilization in household confidence, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect should appear only after HOUST turns up from a depressed base, not during already-strong housing periods.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises month-over-month after being below its 6-month average
failure_condition: Rejected if HOUST rebound triggers are followed by negative or flat SPX returns on average, or if the effect disappears once the 6-month-average condition is imposed.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is already very low and CPIAUCSL is stable, further disinflation can support risk assets by locking in accommodative real rates and suppressing volatility. This mechanism predicts positive one-month equity returns during low-rate, low-inflation conditions and fails when inflation is rising.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS below 1% and CPIAUCSL inflation not accelerating over 3 months
failure_condition: Rejected if positive 20-day SPX returns are not concentrated in periods where both low policy rates and non-accelerating CPI are present, or if rising CPI negates the signal.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent rise in UNRATE during a monetary tightening cycle indicates that policy is biting into employment, which should produce negative equity returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings revisions turn down. The effect is regime-conditional because the same unemployment move is less bearish when policy is easing.
failure_condition: Rejected if rising UNRATE during tightening does not predict negative 60-day SPX returns, or if the sign flips to positive in tightening regimes over rolling walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent slowdown in GDPC1 and RSAFS signals weakening aggregate demand, which should lead analysts to cut revenue and profit forecasts and push the S&P 500 lower over the next quarter. The mechanism is strongest when both real output and nominal spending lose momentum together.
failure_condition: Rejected if simultaneous GDPC1 and RSAFS deceleration fails to predict negative 60-day SPX returns, or if the joint signal does not outperform either proxy independently.
